review_text:
Malheureusement des hommes nous obligés à déposer notre scooter pour nous faire payer un trajet de 100000 idr par personne pour rejoindre le début de la randonnée.
C’est 20minutes de marche donc tout à fait faisable sans avoir à faire à un taxi scooter.

Pour ce qui est du village, la balade en forêt est magnifique, et l’arrivée au village même dans la brume était spectaculaire.
Il est important de savoir qu’il faut venir avec une tenue « correcte » pour les femmes c’est à dire longueur genoux et les épaules couvertes de preference.
Il est bon aussi de savoir qu’il n’est pas recommandé de prendre des photos avant d’avoir l’approbation du chef du village.

On a fait une partie d’échecs avec les ados et joué au foot un petit instant et c’est ce qui a rendu ce moment particulièrement spécial (ou plutôt spécialement normal).

Si vous avez la possibilité de leur amener un jeu d’échecs ils en seront ravis 🤩

N’oublier pas d’acheter une petite bouteille de vin à la cannelle avant de repartir :)

review_text:
An impressive experience !

The trek was no joke though... tough but with many stop points and amazing views.

Once you arrive you are greeted by the village elder who welcomes you in his home.  You are then sent to your sleeping hut with about 30 other people.  Hopefully you don't have anyone who snores!!

Dinner is served around 7pm so if you get up there early you just chill or take pictures.  There are dogs who are friendly as they are your protectors in the night from wild animals.

Breakfast is served at 7am, but if you're lickly get up early and catch the sunrise.

Wonderful place
Luar biasa bisa datang ke tempat ini. Dengan jarak trekking yang lumayan panjang. Ada 7 post yang kita lalui sehingga sampai di kampung adat ini. Harus butuh stamina yang kuat sih untuk mencapai tempat yang indah dan cantik ini.
Wae Rebo yang dikenal dengan desa terindah ke-2 di dunia ini merupakan Kampung adat yang tetap terjaga keaslian akan adat dan kebudayaanya. Dengan warga kampung yang luar biasa ramah sehingga merasa betah seperti di rumah sendiri.
Aseli...... Liburan kali ini paling paling berkesan dan bersyukur bisa ke surga yang tersembunyi ini🥰🥰
author: Fyy serfianti

review_text:
A beautiful experience and very enlightening to see how other people live. Coming from a large city with a corporate job, hectic lifestyle, and a need to always have more, being in this community even for a day has really given me a whole new sense of perspective.

Life here is centred around community, tradition, peace and simplicity. The local people are welcoming and so lovely, you stay in a shared hut with many other visitors, eat simply prepared food and get a snapshot of how this community lives. Grateful I got the opportunity to experience this, and definitely recommend to others.

I went with a tour guide so all transport was organised. The hike to the village takes about 2-2.5 hours and is quite steep and tiring in parts.

The lodgings aren’t overly comfortable, nor are the facilities, but that’s the life here so you do just have to accept and respect it.
author: KDD

review_text:
This was a new experience for me, as I was accompanying my boyfriend’s wish to visit Waerebo. It has always been on my bucket list too, but I was honestly a bit worried knowing we had to hike for 2–3 hours. This time, I pushed myself—and it turned out to be one of the most meaningful experiences.

Arriving there, it instantly felt very close to my heart. The village sits beautifully at the edge of the mountains, surrounded by nature, creating such a peaceful and grounding atmosphere. It felt even more special knowing the history of the place, especially since both me and my boyfriend share Minangkabau roots from our mother’s side.

The Waerebo villagers were incredibly welcoming and friendly. We spent time talking about all sorts of life—sharing stories, experiences, and even our struggles. Some of the villagers had worked in Malaysia for many years just to make ends meet, which made the conversations feel even more personal and relatable. It was a genuine exchange of life and hustle that stayed with me.

Our stay was comfortable, though quite cold up in the mountains. We got to meet and mingle with other tourists and locals visiting the village. One of the highlights was experiencing their traditional music, songs, and dances—it felt very authentic and memorable.

Our dinner and breakfast were prepared by the Waerebo hosts, and we got to enjoy their local food and freshly made coffee from the land itself, which made the experience even more genuine.

It’s truly a beautiful place to visit when you come to Labuan Bajo—something beyond just a trip, but a meaningful journey.

review_text: If you are thinking about visiting make sure you can do the walk in. Good path just a little steep and long…. About 2000 feet or 600 meters from where the mopeds drop you off until marker 5. It took our group 2 hours to walk in. From marker 5 to 6 its flat. The village / valley is gorgeous. Accommodations were rustic but great. Food was simple but traditional and enough. Besides, if you are visiting then you should not be surprised.

review_text: beautiful place, peacefull, and cozy.
author: Bitha Kurniawan

review_text:
The village is very beautiful but also very touristic and the locals don’t sleep in the traditional hits anymore.

Also it is a pity that before starting the trekking there is a public parking but some guys are very aggressive about bringing you there from the bottom with their scooter otherwise they tell you your scooter “is not safe there”. We ended up bribing them with 50k to leave us in peace..
author: Andreu Arderiu

review_text:
Amazing place to hike and enjoy forest covered in fog. Almost no mosquiitos and other bugs. It took max 1.5 hour to hike, first 1 hour with a higher elevation, later - chill walk. In the village we made friends with local guide Amer who was very friendly and spoke good english, had answears to our questions, we felt welcomed.

When we arrived at parking lot local motorbike drivers took us 5-10 min ride to start the hike. They do not let tourist to drive or hike there by themselves. It is a mandatory trip with a porter to the top of the mountain. They did not let us bargain on the price. As we already took a long trip to arrive to parking lot, locals felt in control of the situation because they knew we will go to the mountain anyway. We were informed to pay next day before leaving. So porter 300k idr, motorbike 100k idr both ways.

The hike was not that difficult or confusing so we decided to hike down without a porter, with a broken english we agreed on half of the price and payed him before leaving. All the way down he was going with us, 'leading the way' (even there was only road to the parking lot and we asked him not to), waiting for us when we stop. After we reached parking to place where porters left motorbikes yesterday, they were looking at us angry for not going with them.

After hiking 20 min we reached main parking spot where porter asked us to pay more for leading way down the hill. And we payd because they did not let is go other way.

All experience in the village was not impressive. After welcoming ceremony with mandatory donation (at least 50k) and ground rules, which took 10 min, only activities were to take pictures, eat dinner and breakfast next morning. The food and drinks were tasty, beds okey, simple toilet and friendly tourist. Locals were not interested about us being in the village.

In general, the view was beautiful, hike a little challenging but not worth traveling those roads. Everything is based about tourism, not culture.

review_text: Wae Rebo is a traditional and remote village in the mountains of Flores, Indonesia, famous for its unique, cone-shaped houses called Mbaru Niang. It is a culturally significant site, recognized by UNESCO for preserving the ancestral Manggarai way of life, and offers visitors a chance to experience traditional living and stunning misty mountain scenery. Reaching the village requires a journey that includes an overland drive followed by a multi-hour trek, often with a guide

Siamo arrivati all'inizio del percorso (completamente abbandonato )dopo una strada con i motorini orribile ci affianca in moto una persona in modo minaccioso dicendoci che avremmo dovuto lasciare lì i motorini o addirittura tornare indietro ( al nostro ritorno i motorini erano stati spostati e mi hanno derubato anche di un oggetto che avevo lasciato nascosto vicino al motorino ) si sale in questo percorso che ti fa sembrare davvero di essere in un posto remoto lontano da tutto e tutti dove vive solo l'armonia con la natura. author: Three Blessings

review_text:
5 hours+ of land transport + scary motorbike ride (less than 10 mins) + 2 hours hike to arrive at this World Heritage Village.
What an experience with the broken bridge, road, no network coverage but all nature and cultural heritage.. we are lucky to be able to stay in the Main House although its only few layers of straw mats comparing to mattress in the guest house, we had the honour to shared a chilling night with the head of the Village under one roof. Great scenerary, weather day and night.. milky way.. just relax..
Do expect simple meals (dinner & breakfast) but good coffee from the tribe. Loved the cold shower, feels so good afterwards.
Do bring a jacket as its gets cold at night, a small torch to walk to the toilet/ shower (be mindful with the steps & stairs), disposable raincoat (light to carry for 1 time use), if you to carey many things, can hire a porter (your guide/ assistant/ drive will do a good job)
For myself, i only brought 1 set extra cloth (after shower sleep and next day down hill), small hand towel serve as well as bath towel, i wore water hiking sandal (i slipped down hill on muddy path but it gave me good grip on the stone path)
20 to 25k per coconut is very rewarding at Pos 1 after 1.5hours hike leaving the village. If you can, do swim in the small waterfall as the water is sooooo clean and cold! Crystal clear!!
Take lots of photos!

review_text: We loved out trip to Wae Rebo Village! We booked a 2D1N tour with Green Rinjani Trekking and couldn't be happier with the experience. The walk up is not to hard and the scenery is quite nice, but when you reach the village that is when the view is absolutely breathtaking! When you get there you are not allowed to take any pictures until you got the blessing from the spirits during a short welcome cermony. After that you get to taste some local coffee, take your pictures, interact with the extermly kind locals, learn more abou the village and pet the many very cute dogs. We cam absolutely recommend a trip to the village and staying the night in one of the traditional huts is more then worth it. But please keep in mind that this is a traditional village that you get to experience in an authentic way, so do not expect much luxury and respect their ways! Also we recommend booking a tour with a local guide this way you can learn a lot about the village while supporting their community :)

author: Fitria Indriani

review_text:
My honest, no sugar-coating review here.

You’ve read enough about Wae Rebo in other review. Here’s my review from different perspectives, as a guest/hiker, as a parent, as someone from medical background, to help you set your expectations right.

Points that made the hike worthwhile:
1) the pavement covered about 1/3 of the route. Hats off to the villagers for their efforts on building the pavement in such a challenging, treacherous topography.
2) the village people and kids are really friendly.
3) view are spectacular - you have seen how instagramable it is in the photos.
4) the accommodation provides clean bedding and blanket

Issues of concern:
1) safety
-  hikers do not complain about the basic facilities — this is what we signed up for. The stairs to the outdoor washroom / bath, was  covered in mud, many fell or had a near-fall.
- they could have clear up the muddy stairs to prevent falls.
- Dinner was in another house, it was raining heavily & pitch dark. The ground was muddy and slippery, no lighting/ torchlight was provided. Handphone light wasn’t sufficient in this pitch dark environment. A few of us slipped.
- the person in-charge of tourism could have adopted sustainable energy with solar panel or just a simple gesture of providing a torch light.

2) guest experience
- during the welcome ceremony, we were told by the host this is where we are welcomed like a family member, we will sit and eat together. Fact - you will be eating with groups of hikers, none of the locals will join in. There’s no engagement / conversation with any of the locals. We were rushed through the dinner & breakfast as there was a second batch. If you have been to some nice, small home stay with personalize experience and hospitable hosts, this felt uncomfortable. It felt as if we are being ‘processed’ through instead of hosted as a guest.
- we figured there was easily 120-150 tourists / hikers around. Possibly it’s overcapacity for the village/ hosts to cope. Maybe they should allow respective group to dine in their own house instead of making everyone dine in one house and having to rush us off.

- it was raining heavily at night, We were asked to walk over to the other house for dinner. Our raincoat was broken and our clothes were wet. The host could have offered us umbrella to walk across, instead, they walked off with their umbrella after notifying us of dinner time. Simple gesture as such would have made us feel so much better by not getting wet in the rain again, and slept in cold wet clothes at 12 degree celsius.

3) bring toddlers / kids
- we met some parents with really young toddlers(below 1). I wouldn’t say it’s absolute NO, but this is not the easiest place to hike up and the nearest medical facility / clinic are far out in the village at the base of the mountain. Do weigh in and out the risks.

4) medical / physical limitations
- if you have severe hip , knee arthritis and have trouble with walking longer distance on daily basis/ going up and down the staircase, this place is not for you. The inclination & elevation, are merciless towards your joints. The slippery last 2/3 of the route ( if it rains), is challenging for the balance.

5) what I read from Google review vs my personal experience
- view 5/5
- meal: ok
- experience in the village 3/5

Tips:
1) if you are not a regular hiker with fairly good fitness level but still determined to take this hike, get a guide to pace you.

2) raincoat - get a full-length raincoat from the base of the mountain / the sundry shop in the village.

3) rehydration stick / isotonic drink - you will need this

4) staying dry & warm at night - night temperature at Wae Rebo can be as low as 12 degrees Celsius. Layer up. Pack your clothes in plastic bag / double ziplock bag in case you need to hike in the rain for 2.5 hours like us.

5) stay overnight at Wae Rebo Lodge & hike up early morning to avoid the rain that usually starts by mid noon.

It’s still a beautiful place worth your time as long as expectation is well-managed. Hope this helps. Cheers ~
author: Jacinta Lim

review_text: very unique place
author: Kaila Rahmani

review_text:
Things to note before you come:
1. Good physical condition is required to reach this village, the journey could be arduous depending on your fitness level as the hike may take about 2-3hrs
2. Bring enough cash. There's no digital payment or internet connection along the way to the village, you'll need cash to pay the guide, motorbike taxi, ceremony fee, and staying fee.
3. Bring necessary equipment to hike and staying overnight like spare clothes, toiletries, snacks, water that fit into a backpack.
4. Now there's wifi in the village thanks to starlink, but it's very limited and you have to pay for it, but it's like 20k only, just visit their coffee shop in the village and say you want to use wifi.
Hope it helps on your journey ahead.
author: Danny Kwan

review_text:
It's a paradise village on the mountain.
A very unique 7 traditional "cone-roof" houses.
They only have coffee farm around, so all otherthings daily must carry by motorbike and continue about 2 hours hike by foot.
They only charged 325k for a bed/blanket/pillow in guesthouse for all guests with 2 times meals (dinner and next morning breakfast), 50k for welcome ceremony entered the village area.
Less than 400k rupiah (us$25 / €22) for 2D1N.

IF YOU STILL COMPLAINING ABOUT THAT PRICE,
YOU REALLY A HORRIBLE CHEAPSKATE PERSONALITY!
author: Marko Smith

review_text:
I will share my experience from Dintor to Waerebo. Dintor is the nearest village to Waerebo and it's where all visitors start their journey.

Firstly from Dintor you'll hv to go to the Waerebo trailhead which takes 10-15 mins. A local can send you to this entrance for IDR 50k. You can also ride your own bike there as there's plenty of parking space. The road up there is pretty rough so my advice is just relax and pay someone to send you there. It's not alot of money.

The hike to Waerebo from the entrance is 4.5-5km according to my smartwatch. Waerebo is 1100m above sea. However, the actual hike is only abt 500m elevation gain. It took me 80mins to get to Waerebo from the entrance. From pos5 onwards it's pretty much flat or downhill trail. If you're a trail runner, you can start running from this point onwards. Completing the hike in less than 1 hour is very doable.

If you consistently workout and hike twice a week, 2-3km uphill hikes with minimum 500m elevation then this waerebo hike is a piece of cake. I highly recommend you to do some training before coming here. If you have zero running or hiking experience, then expect to take 3-6 hours.

Waerebo was ok. The fee was IDR 50k for welcome rituals and IDR 200k for food and hangout. Food was so basic I'm tempted to call it lousy. Rice with egg and crackers. I did a day trip because I thought there's probably nothing to do up there aside for taking photos, play with the dogs and daydream. I think they also sell t shirts for IDR 200k - 500k. Can't recall exactly.

If you don't want to be disappointed, I highly recommend you do lots of hiking training before the trip. I can't imagine myself spending 3-4 hours (6-8 return) on a one way hike up just for the pyramid shaped village. I'm quoting 3-4 hours because that's what everyone writes in their reviews.

In the end, I only spent a total of 3+ hours at Waerebo, including hiking up and back to the starting point.
author: Jann Han Lim

review_text: Pemandangan yang sangat indah
author: Fitra Rahmansyah

review_text: This place is very nice and very challenging for those who love nature like me, the first time I went to Wae Rebo with some of my guests it felt like going back to the past because the characteristics of the people in Wae Rebo are very traditional with the traditional customs of Mangarai which makes Wae Rebo village very much in demand by tourists, the existence of the Wae Rebo traditional village which is challenging is because it is located in the middle of the foot of the mountains there are also 7 traditional houses in the shape of a cone surrounding the field or the Mangarai people usually call it natas. we walked from the village by spending 4 hours tracking until we arrived at Wae Rebo village. there the air is very cool and the natural beauty is very pronounced, for nature lovers like me this will be considered like the heaven of Mangarai because of the uniqueness of the village plus the people are very friendly and we are cooled with authentic Wae Rebo coffee.  Even though the journey is very challenging because we have to pass through several bumpy and rocky roads and also have to do tracking, it will all be paid off with the unique and perfect view of the traditional village of Wae Rebo.
author: Astika Pinas

review_text: Butuh perjuangan untuk sampai ke Waerebo. Perjalanan panjang dari labuan bajo sekitar 4- 6 jam. Pendakian butuh 1,5 jam atau lebih. Sedia jas hujan karena sering hujan bila mulai siang dan berkabut.
author: Ria Susmadewi

review_text: Amazing place, wonderful view, humble villagers. It can be a place to calm your body and mind bcs out of crowd and servis. Enjoying millions of stars, cold wind and nature voices
author: Swarman Siahaan

review_text:
Wae Rebo is a beautiful and idyllic village to look at, but unfortunately, it feels more like a money-making operation than a cultural experience. I had expected a genuinely warm welcome and to learn more about the history of the village. Instead, we felt unwelcome, despite the fact that visitors contribute to the local income.

When I respectfully wrapped a cloth around my shorts to show consideration for their culture, I was also met with harsh criticism. The man who welcomed us angrily said, “Show some respect! Think of our values!” even though I believed I was doing exactly that: embracing those very values. It’s a real shame that this gesture wasn’t appreciated but instead criticized.

My friend borrowed a sarong, also to show respect for the culture, its norms and values, but she was laughed at because she didn’t know how to tie it properly. She was then laughed at again for apparently tying it “like a man.” There was little interest in us, and very little help or interaction. The villagers didn’t seem open to engaging and appeared to take no joy in sharing their culture with visitors, which was truly disappointing.

That said, we were still happy to have done the hike and to see the village. But we would recommend skipping the overnight stay, it’s not worth the money and very uncomfortable.
author: Joy Beekelaar

review_text: The best experience ever!!!! 😭✨🔥
author: Agatha O C

review_text: Wisata yg harus dikunjungi di Pulau Flores.
author: Bendvri Silitonga

review_text:
Kalau kamu nyari pengalaman budaya yang autentik dan suasana yang bikin hati adem, Wae Rebo adalah jawaban terbaik! 🏡🌄 Perjalanan menuju desa ini memang bukan buat yang lemah nyali, karena kamu harus trekking sekitar 2,5 sampai 3 jam melewati hutan lebat dan jalur naik turun yang cukup menguras tenaga. Tapi perjalanan itu ibarat investasi yang sangat berharga karena kamu bakal dapetin pengalaman hidup yang sulit dilupakan. 🚶‍♀️⛰️

Setibanya di Wae Rebo, kamu langsung disambut oleh rumah adat Mbaru Niang yang bentuknya unik banget—kerucut tinggi dengan anyaman bambu dan atap daun lontar yang terasa seperti menghubungkan kita ke leluhur. Suasana desa terasa damai dan mistis, apalagi saat kabut turun perlahan, memberi nuansa magis seolah kamu masuk ke dunia lain yang penuh kedamaian dan tradisi. 🌫️🏠

Orang-orang lokalnya ramah dan hangat, mereka dengan senang hati bercerita tentang budaya dan sejarah desa mereka. Kopi lokal yang mereka sajikan juga nggak boleh dilewatkan—aroma dan rasanya benar-benar bikin kamu pengen nambah terus! ☕😍 Malam hari, jangan harap ada suara bising, karena hanya ada suara alam dan gemerlap bintang di langit yang jernih. 🌌✨

Tempat ini benar-benar mengajarkan kita untuk menghargai tradisi dan cara hidup yang sudah ada sejak zaman nenek moyang, sambil menikmati keindahan alam yang luar biasa. Kalau kamu butuh digital detox dan pengen merasakan ketenangan sejati, Wae Rebo adalah surga tersembunyi yang wajib masuk bucket list kamu! 🙌💚
author: Ardhani 2326

review_text:
Hier ein Guide mit allen Infos um im Dorf zu übernachten. Habe mit meiner Freundin hier übernachtet:

Planung:
Keine Reservierung möglich/nötig. Die haben immer genug Betten übrig. Haben, soweit ich weiß, jeden Tag auf.

Anreise:
Mit dem Roller kann man bis zum Google Standort „Wae Rebo Village Trail Head“ fahren. Der letzte Kilometer ist anspruchsvolles Terrain, aber machbar. 1-2 Kilometer vor dem Standort ist ein Haus mit einer offenen Schranke. Die Leute werden versuchen dir einzureden, dass man nicht durchfahren darf. Man darf jedoch durchfahren. Wir sind einfach schnell dran vorbeigefahren ohne anzuhalten. Falls man sich den Weg nicht zutraut kann man sich auch ab dem Haus fahren lassen oder laufen.

Man sollte ca. 13 Uhr mit der Wanderung starten. Diese dauert 2,5 Stunden bis zum Village.

Wir haben zuvor bei „Blasius Monta Homestay“ zu Mittag gegessen (50.000 pro Person). Hier kann man auch Übernachten oder Zeug lagern. Wir haben hier unsere Helme gelagert, weil wir diese nicht mitnehmen wollten beim Wandern.

Am „Warebo Village Trail Head“ sollte man alles ins Helmfach tun und nichts offen liegen lassen.

Ankunft im Dorf:
Wenn man am Ende der Wanderung im Dorf ankommt kann man eigentlich nichts falsch machen.
Man wird gleich abgeholt von einem Einwohner.
Am Anfang gibt es eine Willkommens-Zeremonie. Hier gibt man eine Spende an das Dorf. Unser Hostel meinte 25.000 pro Person sind angemessen.
Die Übernachtung inkl. Abendessen und Frühstück kostet 325.000.

Weiteres:
Im Dorf gibt es keinen Handy Empfang.
Keine Kurzen Hosen, besonders bei Frauen. Im Dorf nur lange Hose. Außerdem ist es auch relativ kalt. Lange Hose & Pulli/Jacke sind optimal.
Man schläft in einem großen Bettenlager ca. 23 Personen, wenn volles Haus. Alles war sauber und in Ordnung. War nichts eklig. Man darf aber auch keinen Luxus erwarten.
Die Einwohner kochen Wasser ab, womit man seine Trinkflasche auffüllen kann.

Fazit:
Wir fanden es ein sehr tolles Erlebnis uns können es weiterempfehlen.
author: Erik

review_text: Desa diatas gunung yg sangat indah dan sangat terjaga.. dari kota labuan bajo harus naik kendaraan sekitar 3-4jam, lalu sampai di parkiran naik ojek ke pos pendakian pertama.. setelah itu tracking kurang lebih 2 jam.. semua terbayar dengan pemandangan yg sangat epiccc
author: Riska Maida

review_text:
Wae Rebo is truly a magical place, a hidden village in the mountains that feels like stepping into another world. Surrounded by lush greenery and misty hills, the beauty of the landscape is absolutely breathtaking. The traditional Mbaru Niang houses are not only unique in architecture but also full of cultural meaning, standing as a symbol of unity and heritage.

What makes Wae Rebo even more special is its people. The community is warm, welcoming, and deeply connected to their traditions. They live in harmony with nature, grow their own coffee and spices, and welcome guests with genuine hospitality. Staying overnight was a humbling experience, sharing meals, joining the evening gathering, and waking up to the peaceful sounds of nature.

This is not just a place to visit, but a way to reconnect with culture, simplicity, and serenity. Highly recommended for anyone seeking an authentic and meaningful experience in Flores.
author: Mohamad Redzuan Mohamad Nizam

review_text: Adem ayeh
author: Adel Mus

review_text: Waerebo, berkendara kurang lebih 5jam untuk sampai desa kaki gunung, lanjut ojek ke pos 1.  Terdapat 7 pos yg di lewati untuk sampai ke sana. Medan yg lumayan, krn pas naik kena hujan. 5kilo dengan waktu stengah jam akhirnya sampe ke desa atas. Dimulai dengan acara adat penyambutan di rumah utama, lanjut disuguhkan kopi hasil panen warga di rumah t4 makan. Pemandangan sangat indah skali, baik menuju malam ataupun di pagi hari. Ke sana bisa ikutan open trip, bisa juga mandiri. Kalau opentrip smua trima beres. Untuk yg mandiri, boleh bawa kendaraan sendiri ataupun nyewa sampai ke parkiran desa kaki gunung, lanjut ngojek warlok 100rb PP, tiket masuk ke desa adat 325rb (include nginap 1 malam dan makan 2 kali), ole² beragam mulai 10rb, ada jg sewa tongkat di bawah 10rb PP. Selain kisah hidup warga Waerebo yg dpt kita pelajari, menikmati alam yg memanjakan mata pun sangat memberi perasaan membahagiakan. Untuk anak² di sana, sebisa mungkin tdk diberi jajan, atau boleh ijin dlu sama orang tua mreka yah, kmarin bawa buku² dan alat tulis ke sana mreka senang trimanya.
author: A. SALMA LARASATI

review_text: This village has been rebuilt years ago. It is not the original. The real village is in another side where it has road connections and schools. In conclusion, this is just a replica of the old ways and living in such huts. It is 3 hours 1 way hike up and a motorbike ride of 15 mins. Got to knock bamboo bell to inform village chief upon your arrival. Later, they will bless you in the main hut. Everyone will stay, sleep and eat together with no privacy in another hut at 8pm. There is only electricity from 6pm to 10pm where you can charge your power banks and battery. There is a warung at the entrance where they sell instant noodles, tea and coffee. There is paid wifi there. Everyone got to eat together, food isn't that fantastic. There is no meat but vegs. It is just for the experience. There are 3 toilets cum bathroom and are really basic. No hot water. At night, mosquitoes will visit you  when you sleep at 10pm where all lights are out and everyone snores. Prep your phones lights or headlamps. Good for 1 night stay at most. I call this tourist entertainment.
author: Lee Kokliang

review_text: Rumah adat wae rebo
author: Rafael Andries Tino

review_text:
Tourist scam, foreigners have to pay way too much (compared to Indonesian) and you're not allowed to actually explore the village or talk to the locals
If you just wanna have an insta pic, go for it - otherwise go i.e. to Bena Village in Bajawa
author: Maximilian Lang

review_text:
Kamoung adat di kabupaten Manggarai Flores, yang special, perjalanan trekking menjadikan tempat ini semakin menarik, setelah menyusuri hutan akhirnya bertemu kampung kecil dwngan bentuk bangunan yang unik. Tradisi yang selalu terjaga.  Membuat perjalanan ke kampung ini menjadi kenangan  perjalanan yang indah.
Semoga selalu terjaga keasrian hutan dan budayanya
author: hasrizal

review_text:
Absolutely avoid during the rainy season!

The trek turned into a nightmare as a tropical downpour fell on us during 1 hour of the trek, the path became extremely dangerous and we arrived drenched despite wearing rain gear (our stuff still isn't dry 48h later and our phones and tablets could have died).

The hike is nice and the village is beautiful, but that's where it ends. Prices are extremely expensive to visit the village or to stay and eat there, and none of the locals interact with the tourists (let's not even mention the "welcome ceremony of the village chief", which is a real joke). They try to extort money from tourists at all costs (example: sarong rental 100.000 !!!).

Overall, a rather disappointing experience
author: Estelle Platiau

review_text:
Tempatnya sangat memuaskan mata, walaupun perjalanan dari pos pertama hingga pos 7 atau pos terahir sangatlah jauh atau melelahkan bayangkan 1200 DPL? Itu sangat tinggi bukan, tapi semua itu akan terbayarkan jika sudah melihat betapa cantiknya desa WAE REBO🥰🥰
Buat yg belum pernah kesini, wajib coba deh😍😍👍
author: Erfin Samsop

review_text:
Besides the uniqueness of the culture, experience, and local politeness;
It is by far the best stargazing place. Especially when all lights went off...
author: Alfian Noor

review_text: Balade très agréable, village très beau ; pour le reste c'est du racket sur fond pseudo folklorique, sans compter l’énorme perte de temps, la cérémonie est une vaste blague simple excuse pour prendre un petit billet supplémentaire, la nuit et le repas sont similaires à un escape game grotesque et bon marché, tous entassés au sol dans une case à ingurgiter un repas maigre et insipide sous la surveillance d’un local debout le visage fermé ... Je ne vais pas m’étendre plus sur le sujet de crainte de devenir vraiment désagréable et bien des commentaires avant le mien font état des désagréments rencontrés. Si vous envisagez d’y aller faites le par vos propres moyens, sans pseudo guide qui ne sert à rien, et surtout ne tombez pas dans le piège de rester la nuit, faites en sorte de faire l’aller-retour dans la journée.
author: Pablo

review_text:
Great experience. I went there with Happy Bajo because I’m traveling solo and not in the mood to drive a motorbike alone there. But I saw some people go there by their own and the village people still welcomed them and you can pay them directly.

The pathway to the village is around 5 km with the last 2 km is relatively flat or downhill but it was worth the sweat.

The village is very beautiful, the people are nice. I slept like a baby in one of the hut, I even took a bath in with water from the spring water, cold but it was very refreshing after the hike.

I regret not buying coffee from them. I bought bracelets from the cute kids.
author: Barbara

review_text: Disappointed. The whole “experience” felt like a scam. First you need a local guy (sorry, “guide”) to take you to the village, it’s 300.000, although if you’re Indonesian from another island you can pass freely 😐 then you pay 50.000 for the ceremony where they tell you close to nothing about the village and lecture you not to speak to anyone in the village and not to interact with the animals, not to take pictures and a few more “not to”, after that they shove you into another hut with some food and coffee being served and invite you to join. I thought that was nice and welcoming but after I was done I got a bill for 200.000 🥲 (the most expensive rice, omelette and boiled cucumbers in my life). Again, if you’re Indonesian you don’t have to pay  for food either 👍 I brought sweets and origami for the kids in the village hoping to play with them a little and wasn’t even allowed to give it to them directly. Ended up spending 30 min in the village and left. Not recommended. So many similar villages around the island with easier access and more welcoming people, go to Bajawa instead.
author: Katherine

review_text:
Wae Rebo Traditional Village is one of the most famous and most frequently visited traditional villages on Flores Island.

This village is one of many Manggarainese villages in the Greater Manggarai region that still maintains the authenticity of the village as well as its houses formation that inherited from their ancestor for hundreds year. Like other traditional Manggarainese villages, this village consists of Mbaru Gendang or a main traditional house at its center and other traditional houses surrounding on its left and right side so together they form a circular layout. In front of the main house there is a Compang or altar stone that is commonly used in many of rituals to worship their ancestor's spirit.

The uniqueness of Wae Rebo Traditional Village that distinguishes it from other traditional villages is its location lies deep in the valley of Satar Mese mountainous area which can only be reached by trekking.

Accessibilities: To access Wae Rebo Traditional Village, first we need to get into the modern village nearby called Dintor Village, Satar Lenda District, Manggarai Regency, which is about 50km/1.5 hours from Ruteng City or about 100km/3 hours from Labuan Bajo City using motorcycle or car. Half of the roads lead to Dintor are wide and good, the rest are small and badly constructed & maintained. Once we arrived, we have to continue to walk for about 6 km / 2.5 hours from the uphill of modern village nearby. The trekking path lead to Wae Rebo is clear but quite narrow, risky, also draining energy due to the steep of the hill.

Amenities: There are many amenities available around Dintor such as markets, food and beverage vendors, and lodging. Around Wae Rebo, there is only one traditional house that is specifically intended for tourists to stay all in one with breakfast, food and drinks, and also a toilet. For electricity, in Dintor it is always available connected to the power plant, while in Wae Rebo it is not always available only from 6 PM to 12 PM powered by small local generator. For water, there is no need to worry because there are many water springs on the mountain that is always flowing abundantly to all villages.
author: Aswin Nobby

review_text: Это уникальное место, которое точно стоит посетить. Для полного погружения в атмосферу, почитайте книгу об этой деревне в гостевом доме, а также можно пообщаться с жителями и задать им разные вопросы об их жизни - это будет увлекательный диалог. Рекомендую подниматься с рассветом (или ночевать наверху) - с утра будет меньше облаков и меньше туристов, которые большими толпами приходят с 10-11 утра и позже. До парковки нужно доехать на байке, далее пешком идти 2 часа. В самой деревне нужно сначала дать небольшое символичное пожертвование за церемонию очищения, а далее оплатить вход 200К с человека (в него входит обед).
author: Ivan

review_text: Pokok nya seru deh
author: bumi Tangge

review_text:
Something unique. Worth visiting.
We went up with Komodo Blessing Adventures tours and it was great!

Tapi mereka, orang kampung Waerebo butuh beruba semua lampu putih itu untuk warm white... di malam tidak catik lagi. Harus improve itu... jangan pake lampu putih aja lampu kuning yang kurang keras untuk mata.
author: Paula Huerta

review_text: Desa terindah yang pernah saya kunjungi, mulai dari viewnya, suasanya dan juga kopinya yang sangat enak sekalihhhh, pokokkknya harus balik lagi ke waerebo🥹🔥🔥
author: Rivan

review_text:
La experiencia de ir a Waerebo, en general, me ha gustado. Son 2 h y 20 minutos de caminata en subida y 1h y 40 minutos de bajada. Les falta aún un poco de atención al turista. Al llegar no nos explicaron nada, solo hicieron una ceremonia de bienvenida que consistió en que el jefe dedica unas palabras en indonesio. No hacen ceremonias ni bailes para los turistas. Sólo las hacen cuando corresponde la celebración del acto por fecha. Una pena. Aún así, muchas noches tocan los tambores y platos para pasarselo bien. Además, también puedes entrar en cada casa y ver como viven: dónde duermen, cocinan, etc. Se dedican mayoritariamente a la producción de café y manualidades varias como sarungs. Por la mañana se ponen temprano a preparar las lonas llenas de café para que seque y para moler los granos en el mortero que tienen delante de las casas.
Ver la salida del sol con este paisaje es increíble.
La comida muy escaso: arroz, espinacas, patatas de gamba, 1 huevo duro y bananas. El desayuno es arroz y un trozo de tortilla.
CONSEJOS:
1) llevar snacks por si os quedáis con hambre o incluso para el trekking.
2) llevar chanclas para pasear por allí. Se os hará más comodo porque para entrar en las casas te tienes que sacar los zapatos y el baño siempre está mojado.
3) repelente de mosquitos.
4) es obligatorio llevar al menos una camiseta de manga corta y pantalon largo como símbolo de respeto a la comunidad.
author: Rosa Maria Rovira Claveras

review_text: Wae Rebo dengan keunikannya. Buat kamu yang tidak tahan dengan suhu yang dingin jangan lupa jaket ya,,
author: Priska Prylia

review_text: Magnifique! Certes touristique, droit d'entrée, case pour dormir à plusieurs, montée ardue, route évidemment comme dans l'est de Flores cabossées ....mais voir les étoiles depuis ce petit village perdu, de lever au petit matin et contempler la brume qui le noie reste magique. Certes les villageois sont blasés par l'afflux des touristes et vivent leur vie , semblant indifférents. Comment leur reprocher de tirer avantage dans un pays si pauvre. Mais si on prend la peine de s'y accommoder tout doucement, de prendre juste place à leurs côtés ils sont très accueillants. Ce n'est que mon avis personnel mais je vous conseille d'y aller pour la beauté qui s'en dégage sans forcément attendre milles échanges avec eux.
author: Nirmala

review_text: tempat yang luar biasa indah dan nyaman dengan nuansa pedesaan dibawah kaki gunung. Walaupun motoran sekitar 5 jam dari Labuan Bajo melewati jalur Lembor dan tracking sekitar kurang lebih 2 jam, tapi semuanya terbayarkan sama keindahannya dan masyarakat disini juga ramah-ramah. Suhu di Wae rebo juga lumayan dingin dan berkabut. Tempat yang sangat recommended untuk melepas penat dan mencari experience baru tinggal dengan suasana pedesaan.
author: Gabriella Natalie

review_text: beautiful place😍
author: nadia mayesa

review_text: Keren banget pokoknya,pengen kesini lagi
author: Anselmus Roung

review_text: AMAZING AMAZING experience. From Labuan 4hr drive & 2hr hike. After arriving we had dinner (rice, eggs & veggies) and met the local tribe members in their hut who were very friendly and welcoming. At night they provide a bed, pillow and blanket. Breakfast was provided in the morning (rice and eggs). It was a very comfortable experience in the village. Would recommend to everyone however it is not accessible. Hike is long but was not bad (we had a 8yo local doing it with us in his flip flops).
author: Rezinhaaa

review_text:
Siapkan fisik dan materi apalagi kalo kamu jauh dr jakarta.
Tempatnya indah bangeeettt.
Pokonya kamu haru kesini minimal sekali seumur hidup
author: Syifa erdhe

review_text:
Beautiful small village in the middle of mountain, enjoy the night with no light pollution, you can see clear sky full of stars.

For ticket information you can stay in the house pay around Rp330k/$25 for one night, and don't forget bring cash money.

You can bring your snacks and share with local kids
author: riki afifuddin

review_text: Der Ort ist sehr touristisch geprägt,  obwohl acht Familien dort wohnen. Der Anmarsch besteht aus 5km Motorradfahrt und 3h Aufstieg zum Dorf. Bei Ankunft gibt es eine zwei Minuten Zeremonie. Abstieg dauert 2h plus Motorradfahrt. Die Übernachtung in der "romantischen Rundhütte" entpuppte sich als 20 Betten Schlafsaal. Alkohol und Rauchen sind verboten. Zu kaufen gibt es nur am Eingang Wasser und Softgetränke, keine Kekse. Bei Bedarf Reserven mitnehmen,  sowie Wechselwäsche. Frühstück und Abendessen sind gut und ausreichend.
author: Andy Her

review_text:
I would advise to hike from morning so you would arrive at least at the top still in the afternoon.

Especially if you are not an active sporty person, you might want to take it slow when hiking, like me, I took around 4 hours arrived at the village. I started at around 2 pm and arrived around 6 pm, it's started foggy around 4.30 pm. It was very beautiful though foggy forest with many big and small trees (you even can find coffee trees on the sides). There were really big bamboo trees!

If I remember it correctly there are 3 posts. From the starting point to first post, it's a paved rocky road, easy to step in but pretty steep, at least for me.

From 1st to 2nd post you will hike on unpaved ground. Be careful when it's raining, can be slippery. I would suggest you rent a stick to help you keep your balance.
2nd to 3rd post, the track was pretty similar, but the trees were bigger, so giving a better shade from the sun. I did not even feel hot. You will breath a clean air the deeper you go up to the village.
3rd to the village, there were part where the walk path was getting narrower. But you could still across it.
author: silvia oktarina

review_text: Bagus view dan pelayanannya
author: Irsach Karll Ardans

review_text:
Das sollte man vorher Wissen! Ca. 7 1/2 Kilometer und 800 Höhenmeter größtenteils steiler Anstieg auf befestigten und unbefestigten Weg.
Die Anstrengung wird mit einem schönen Ausblick auf das Dorf belohnt
Die Übernachtung für 325.000 im Schlafsaal ist Geschmackssache

review_text: Ada biaya yang harus dikeluarkan sebesar 325.000 per orang untuk menginap dan 50.000 per grup yang diserahkan pada saat upacara penyambutan. Dapet makan malam dan makan pagi. Menunya nasi, sayur, telur, kerupuk. Kerupuk talasnya enak. Bisa dibeli juga yang Mateng atau mentah seharga 25.000 sekantong kecil. Ada toilet duduknya
author: Dwi Yasunah

review_text: Ferry good
author: Efan Nopang (Efan)

review_text: Amazing experience. A very bumpy 10 mins The scooters to get to the start of the hike and then the hike up for the first half is very strenuous so make sure you're fit and healthy. Once there it's nice to disconnect and relax. The food is mainly rice and egg with some veg which is nice. When I was up there we saw pig sacrifices which was interesting to see.
author: Rhys Davies

review_text: Stunning village, looks just like the photos. Opportunity to spend the night for 350k including dinner and breakfast. Don’t bother hiring a guide for the hike, it’s easy and took me 1h45 to do alone. Be prepared for the most gruelling drive to get there. The road is AWFUL, wouldn’t recommend unless you’re an experienced driver. Also bear in mind the drive from Labuan Bajo said 3h on Google maps but took me 6h!!! Cos of the state of the roads it takes basically double the time. Was a long day of bumpy driving :(
author: Anya Claire

review_text: Ini merupakan wisata kampung di atas awan yang saya kunjungi

review_text: Great place, good people, beautiful scenary. Worth visiting
author: Dahlia Halimuko

review_text: Good

review_text: kalo ke bajo wajib ke sini
author: eko hosmi

review_text: Visitare questo luogo incantevole significa immergersi nel puro stile di vita indonesiano di un tempo ( ma anche recente ). Ci si arriva dopo circa 2.5 ore di cammino attraversando una affascinante foresta. Il sentiero in parte rifatto durante la pandemia è ben tenuto nonostante le piogge. Per chi dorme al villaggio si deve accontentare di una capanna dormitorio / ristoro comune con altre persone. Tutte le capanne sono all’interno avvolte da un fumo generato dai bracieri interni utilizzati sia per cucinare che per affumicare appositamente l’interno in modo da preservarlo nel tempo. Si mangia per terra e i bagni sono quel che sono. Consiglio di portarsi una zanzariera e repellente utile per la notte. Il tutto è molto “wilde” ma a mio avviso esperienza unica! Dimenticavo…Producono un ottimo caffè!
author: Giramondo

review_text: tracking 2 jam yg terbayarkan dengan sangat puas, 2 jam itu termasuk sangat cepat sampai yah.  untuk teman" yg mau kesini upayakan lari" h-3 sebelum kesini soalnya jalurnya menguras tenaga. motor bisa sampai parkiran atas dengan tanjakan yg lumayan dan beberapa titik di jalan kurang bagus. dan minesnya di desa terakhir warganya maksa agar kita naik ojek dengan tarif 100k perorang.
author: Khairul Imam

review_text: Rumah adat yang sangat cantik 😘😘
author: Marselina Ani

review_text: Journey to the paradise, indah banget kampung ini
author: yunan morfosis

review_text:
Die Fahrt von Labuan Bajo mit unserem privat-Fahrer war sehr gut! Tolle Landschaft, unterwegs schöne Zwischenstopps.
Das Dorf an sich, welches zum Unesco Weltkulturerbe zählt ist auch recht schön, allerdings dem Massentourismus verfallen.
Wir waren recht enttäuscht und fühlten uns “ ausgenommen”.
Klar das es was kostet, jedoch wird für jede Kleinigkeit extra berechnet.
Wir hatten Glück, dass unser lokaler Fahrer moderatere Preise für uns ausgehandelt hatte.
Die Wanderung nach oben ist sehr schön.
Sind nachmittags rauf und morgens vor dem inkludierten Frühstück wieder nach unten gegangen.
Abendessen : Reis mit Brühe und einem gekochten Ei.

Unbedingt genügend Wasser mitnehmen!
Idealerweise einen Hüttenschlafsack und trockene Wechsel Kleidung dabei haben.

Fazit: teuerstes Matratzenlager unserer gesamten Indonesien -Reise
author: alpenkind

review_text: Sangat bagus
author: Venan Hadu

review_text:
Quelle déception!

Je ne sais pas par où commencer tellement j’ai été déçu par cet endroit.
Le village en lui même est joli mais est devenu une machine à cash, ce qui enlève tout son charme: vous payez 50000 IDR en arrivant pour la cérémonie d’accueil, qui n’est rien d’autres que 2 phrases que le chef du village prononce pour demander votre protection durant votre présence (elle dure entre 5 et 10 secondes). On vous emmène ensuite à la « guesthouse » pour vous faire payer l’entrée au village qui coûte 200000 IDR par personne; ce prix inclut le droit de rester dans le village pour la journée ainsi que le repas de 12h et un café (bon a savoir si vous prévoyez de réaliser l’ascension à l’aube pour redescendre au fin de matinée). Le repas que nous avons eu était compose de riz blanc, d’un bouillon de légumes et d’une omelette, très décevant vis à vis du prix global payé.
Il est également possible de passer une nuit à la guesthouse et de repartir le lendemain matin pour 325000 IDR par personne (dîner et petit déjeuner inclus), mais vous dormirez dans une grande pièce avec tous les autres touristes. A titre d’exemple lorsque nous avons visité le village, 48 personnes avaient dormi au village la veille, je vous laisse faire les calculs et vous représenter l’authenticité du lieu.

La randonnée pour accéder au village est assez dure (1h40 pour monter avec 2 pauses pour des personnes avec une bonne condition physique) mais nous avons eu la chance de croiser des singes à l’aller et au retour, qui n’hésitent pas à s’approcher assez près pour vous observer.

De plus, la route pour accéder à la ville de denge (au pied de la montagne) est CATASTROPHIQUE que ce soit depuis labuan bajo ou depuis ruteng. La route est principalement constituée de galets (voir photo) et il y a énormément de trous et de gravillons. Comptez au moins 1h de trajet avec ces conditions d’un côté ou de l’autre. Enfin, pas de possibilité de trouver du carburant sur ces routes. Il faudra en prendre avant, ou à denge pour 25000 IDR la bouteille.

Au niveau du parking à scooter au départ de la randonnée, des locaux malhonnêtes nous ont demandé 20000 IDR de parking au retour (nous avions commencé l’ascension à 7h et personne n’était présent), ne payez pas ce sont juste des conducteurs de taxi moto qui attendent leurs clients.

Bref, en résumé cela prend beaucoup de temps et d’argent pour quelque chose de décevant, ça devait être sympa au début mais c’est devenu Disneyland. D’autres villages plus accessibles peuvent peut être valoir davantage le coup.

Ps: Pas besoin de prendre un guide, nous n’en avons pas eu besoin et ceux que nous avons croisé ne donnaient pas vraiment d’informations à leurs clients.
author: Pierre-Yves Gielen

review_text:
Mauvaise expérience dans sa globalité. Une visite très moyenne qui nécessite pourtant beaucoup de temps et d’argent.

Concernant la route pour arriver à la ville de Dintor/Denge : le sentier côtier est difficilement praticable, en scooter cette partie nous a pris 2h sur la partie ouest (direction Labuan Bajo) et 1h sur la partie est (direction Ruteng).

Concernant les environs du village Wae Rebo :
Les logements dans la ville de Dintor/Denge sont chers par rapport à la qualité des installations (400k à 500k pour un couple pour une nuit avec dîner et petit déjeuner).
De plus, nous avons eu de nombreuses mauvaises expériences avec les habitants de Dintor/Denge et de la route vers l’ouest jusque Lembor. Des chauffeurs de taxi motos nous demandent 20k de stationnement alors que c’est gratuit, vol d’effets personnels sur le scooter, prix de l’essence augmenté en raison de notre origine, enfants qui ne cessent de demander de l’argent,…
Comportements qui tranchent avec le reste du voyage, pendant lequel nous avons expérimenté l’accueil et la bienveillance des indonésiens.

Concernant la visite du village traditionnel de Wae Rebo : Depuis le point référencé « Wae Rebo Village Trail Head » sur Google Maps et accessible en scooter, la randonnée pour le village a duré 1h40 de montée et 1h30 de descente (saison sèche). La première partie de la randonnée (30 min) est aménagée et très raide, la seconde partie (1h) est un sentier de terre et pierre qui montent beaucoup moins mais est légèrement glissant (attention dans la descente).
Une fois le café passé, si vous descendez au centre du village traditionnel, vous devez vous présenter et payer l’entrée.
Il y a deux façons de visiter le village :
-la « visite » en journée pour laquelle vous payez 200k pour l’entrée et 25k pour la « cérémonie d’accueil » du chef du village (1 courte phrase prononcée assis dans la maison principale). Vous aurez un café d’accueil et un déjeuner basique (œuf, riz, bouillon).
-la nuit dans le village pour laquelle vous payez 325k pour l’entree, la cérémonie, le les repas et la nuit.
Lors de notre visite en août 2024, 12 personnes ont fait la visite de journée et 48 personnes ont dormi dans le village la veille.
Il n’y a pas d’interaction avec les habitants qui voient défiler des touristes en continu et n’ont peut être plus l’envie de partager.
Une vieille dame attend qu’on l’a photographie pour ensuite demander d’acheter des bracelets en souvenir.
Le tourisme a certainement transformé la vie des habitants du village, il est difficile de savoir ce qu’il reste des traditions, si ce n’est les 7 maisons conservés et entretenus.
L’endroit est néanmoins très beau, le cadre montagneux et l’architecture des maisons en font un endroit atypique.
Les touristes qui n’ont pas souhaité payer les frais d’entrée, qu’ils considéraient trop élevés pour un passage rapide, ont été repoussés en haut du village traditionnel.
author: MICHEL Eva

review_text:
Paradiso fuori dal mondo.
La fatica per arrivarci ne vale la pena.
author: Santax Frank

review_text: Money making gimmic
author: Sabrina Lakhan

review_text: Hidden gem. Desa wisata exclussive, karena butuh effort khusus untuk bisa kesana. Arsitektur unik bangunan, timeless
author: bonita santi

review_text:
Waerebo, sebuah desa adat terpencil dan misterius di Kabupaten Manggarai, Nusa Tenggara Timur. Terletak di ketinggian 1.200 meter di atas permukaan laut. Di kampung ini terdapat 7 rumah utama atau yang disebut sebagai Mbaru Niang.
Untuk sampai ke lokasi butuh perjuangan ekstra karena harus jalan kaki 1-2 jam,
Siapkan air secukupnya saat tracking,
Bila perlu gunakan tongkat sebagai alat bantu,
Tempatnya sangat indah, nyaman dan tenang penuh kedamaian
author: Jackson Nh

review_text:
Wae Rebo salah satu desa adat tercantik yang pernah aku datangi.
Perjalanan panjang dari Labuan Bajo terbayar dengan pemandangan indah nya,
Pengalaman pertama aku Trekking 5km, rekomendasi untuk orang yang bener2 cinta nature, semoga keindahan alam nya akan selalu terjaga
author: Mellisa Christine

review_text: Bercengkrama dan berolahraga santai dengan anak-anak desa Waerebo
author: Mulyadi Supanjang

review_text: Muy buena experiencia, hay locales abajo que cobran estacionamiento (es gratis!) arriba hay que pagar 200.000 por la ceremonia, a un buen ritmo sin parar son 50 minutos
author: nicolas delano

review_text: Perjalanan yang sangat menyenangkan dan  bertemu dg orang-orang yang luar biasa hangat dan bersahabat
author: ismaniar syafrudin

review_text:
For the people who want to do the trekking by themselfs. It is an very easy to follow trekking. In our opinion there is no need for guides. We did it by ourselfs. The first part is paved but slippery and steep. And the second part is unpaved but less steep.
We did it in 1:40 up an 1:30 down.

With an scooter you can drive to Dende, there is a car parking. Thereafter you can drive a bit with a scooter uphill, the road is paved but full of putholes and loose gravel. If good experienced you can manage.

Local guides where hanging on our scooter when we came back.

Come as early as possible! Less people and also no locals asking for money.
author: Jasper dW

review_text: Beautiful traditional houses in the middle of the forest and hills. Entrance fee is 50.000 IDR which you pay after a "ceremony" which is a short talk where the village head asks his ancestors for permission to enter the village. You can also sleep overnight with diner and breakfast for 325.000 IDR. Then you sleep with 25 people in a traditional house. The diner and breakfast was disappointing: rice with egg. It was also very disappointing that there was not any initiative from the local villagers to tell something or show something about their village. There is a small shop that sells their locally produced products: coffee, vanilla, cinnamon, honey, and wine. To reach this village you must walk about 75 minutes (the first half is the steepest part). There are 3 rest points on the way.
author: Travel Here Youtube

review_text: Trecking 2,5 - 3 jam, jalan trecking melalui hutan dan menanjak. Suhu malam hari dingin sekali, sorenya berkabut, banyak turis asingnya. Syahdu sekali bikin betah. Beli kopi dari petani langsung 1 cangkir kopi bubuk arabika harga 50 rb. Kopinya enaaaak.
author: Lintang Cahya A

review_text: Tempat yang sangat bagus.
author: Oktavianus Tosima

review_text: Luar biasa suasana di Wairebo, masih alamai
author: Darius Uren

review_text: Beware, we were food poisoned after staying at the village.
author: Alize Ml

review_text: Great village with great people.
author: Arief Rahman Hasyim

review_text: Wonderful place
author: Edy Santosa

review_text: At first it was raining which made the trek less enjoyable, the road was long and boring.  When I got to the top, it was good but the cloudy and rainy weather made it look worse. Then we went inside the main hut, I wasn't too bad but kinda boring. When we got 1 hut per about 30 people. There was dinner, but it was with Indonesian food only and some tea. The bathroom was pretty dirty and there were leeches. When I went to bed like 3-4 people were snoring and I could barely sleep. The next day it was sunny but everything was still wet. The trek back down was still boring. Nothing changed of course.
author: Limbo Key :3

review_text: Kopi Waerebo terbaik
author: Michael Waerebo

review_text:
We loved our stay at Waerebo Village, it took us 2 hours to hike up and we were welcomed with a traditional ceremony. We stayed the night and felt the 325k fee for board, 3 meals and the experience was well worth it. The villagers were kind and welcoming and it was interesting to be part of their community for a night.

It’s a shame there’s a few bad reviews on this place, the village has been adapted to allow tourists to stay and it’s a big journey from either Labuan Bajo or Ruteng so I’m unsure why people make the effort just to moan about the cost (things can’t be provided free of charge)
author: Jessica Tait

review_text: Incredible experience and very good value for money in my opinion! Great to get an insight into traditional ways of living in Flores
author: Alex higgs

review_text: Mantap, pengalam pertama kami tracking 5KM dgn medan yg berbeda dr biasa bagi kami. Desa yg asri n kental akan adat dan yg di akui UNESCO. Sgt rekomen utk memgunjungi nya guys
author: Oka Oprinda

review_text: Did the hike in 1.5 hours and arrived there for lunch time. You are welcome with a ceremony from the leader of the village (recommend giving 25K/person for donation) and then you are part of the community and can enjoy at the village. Everyone was really nice and welcoming, we learned a lot about Manggarai culture and the experience is just incredible. You pay 325k/person to sleep there including lunch, diner and breakfast it’s worse it and it helps the community 🙏🏼
author: Lexi

review_text:
Si vous recherchez de l'authentique, fuyez.
Sympa pour faire une randonnée, même si 200000 rp + la donation à l'arrivée , ça commence à faire cher.
Arrivez vers 9 h, les touristes  de la nuit et les influenceurs seront partis et vous serez plus tranquille.
author: Marie Baumgarth

review_text: Kereen 👍👍
author: Syarifuddin Uddin

review_text:
Salah satu desa terindah di dunia ada di Indonesia yaitu desa Waerebo. Untuk mencapai kesini bisa lewat Ruteng atau Labuan Bajo. Saya memulai perjalanan dari Labuan Bajo sehingga membutuhkan kira-kira 6 jam perjalanan. 4 jam naik mobil, 30 menit naik ojek dan 1 jam 30 menit mendaki. Pengalaman perjalanannya cukup seru. Dimulai dari naik mobil yang sempat nyebrang air payau. Naik ojek di bukit dan turunan terjal, hingga trekking. Saya salut sekali sama penduduk sini mulai dari anak kecil hingga lansia yang sehari hari trekking disini. Saya juga salut sama keluarga wisatawan yang bawa anak kecil usia 10 tahunan, kuat loh mereka trekking.

Di Waerebo kita akan tinggal di penginapan yang sudah disediakan. Sejauh yang saya tahu ada 2 rumah dengan kapasitas sekitar 20 orang. Tidak ada kamar privat jadi lebih ke satu rumah besar octagon, di pinggirnya dijejeri kasur dan di tengah ada space kosong untuk lesehan makan atau ngobrol.

Disini yang penting jaga sikap sewajarnya aja sih, warga Waerebo adalah orang orang yang ramah, cerdas dan rajin. Saya sempat ke salah satu rumah dan penghuni nya kira-kira ada 7 kepala keluarga. Semuanya ramah dan sangat antusias bercerita. Kebetulan di rumah itu ada yang tertua namanya nenek Katarina. Nenek Katarina masih aktif dan sehat.

Karena keterbatasan waktu, saya hanya 1 hari 1 malam. Kalau misalnya mau merasakan gaya hidup damai di desa mungkin harus lebih lama lagi. Ohiua disini tidak ada sinyal, jadi ya kita bakal meresapi hidup banget disini.
author: Oemar Ichsan

review_text: You better be ready for the steep hike in the first half. The hike took us more or less about 1.5 hrs. Took my parents 4 hrs, but they did it. Is it worth it? Damn right it is. There's a house guardian cat too. Super friendly cat.
author: Ry Wijaya

review_text: floress
author: Adam Prasetyo (verified)

review_text:
Perjalanan dari Labuan Bajo menuju Dintor selama +/-5 jam menggunakan mobil, lanjut menuju Kampung Denge.

Dari kampung Denge naik ojek menuju start point trekking. Trekking kurang lebih selama 2.5 jam, jika mau menggunakan tongkat ada sewa tongkat 10rb.

Ada beberapa pos pemberentian, jika sudah di pos terakhir, membunyikan kentongan sebagai tanda tamu datang. Sesampai di desa, akan ada upacara penerimaan tamu, setelah upacara baru dapat mengambil foto dll.

Ada sebuah altar berbentuk bulat yang tidak boleh disentuh/dinaiki. Listrik di desa ini menggunakan genset, dinyalakan sampai  jam 22.00, jadi sebelum jam 22:00 dapat mengecas HP.

Jika menginap kita akan disuguhi makan malam dan makan pagi. Jenis makanan berupa nasi, telur, sayur, dan kerupuk. Sederhana karena keterbatasan akses, tetapi rasanya cukup enak.

Keindahan Desa Waerebo sangat magical, malam hari bintang sangat jelas terlihat, di pagi hari melihat keindahan sunrise.

Disana juga dapat berinteraksi dengan penduduk lokal, beberapa penduduk menjual kerajinan tangan berupa gelang, gantungan kunci.
author: Yuliana Linda

review_text:
Kayaknya aku bisa bilang bahwa Waerebo ini adalah salah satu desa adat tercantik yang pernah aku lihat. Perjalanannya lumayan menantang, mulai dari perjalanan naik mobil yang rutenya sulit dan banyak jalan rusak. Sesampai di sana kita juga mesti naik ojek motor sebelum sampai di titik awal hiking, dilanjut lagi hiking sekitar 7km di jalan yang banyak menanjaknya. Aku butuh waktu sekitar 2.5 jam untuk naik, kalau turun relatif lebih cepat karena jalannya juga menurun yah.

Di sana tidurnya dalam 1 rumah besar jadi tidurnya ramai2 dengan pengunjung/turin lainnya. Makanan yg dihidangkan juga relatif sederhana, wajar karena mereka sendiri untuk bawa bahan dengan rute sesulit itu aja lumayan yah. Jadi siapkan cemilan atau makanan tambahan aja.
author: Sharron Sharron

review_text:
This was a very exciting and fun experience in my life when traveling to Wae Rebo village. The traditional village which is located above 1,200 meters above sea level makes Wae Rebo village dubbed as a village above the clouds, and the journey to get here takes 3-5 hours with uphill terrain. In this village it is very calm and soothing, to get here you need to be assisted by an experienced tour guide. This village is very beautiful to visit. Waerebo traditional village is famous for its stunning natural beauty and the existence of the unique and interesting Mbaru Niang traditional house.

In addition, Waerebo Traditional Village also offers a unique experience for tourists who want to experience the traditional life of the Flores people. Travelers can take part in various activities such as cooking traditional food, learning to weave, and participating in traditional ceremonies. With its natural beauty and cultural diversity, Waerebo Traditional Village is an attractive tourist destination for visitors who want to experience traditional Indonesian life. At least once in a lifetime you can visit this village. I hope to visit again soon!!!🥰🥰
author: Ando Emanuel

review_text: The village of Wae Rebo in Flores is absolutely stunning 🤗The views are incredible with traditional cone-shaped houses surrounded by green mountains. The locals were very friendly and welcomed us warmly, making our experience even more memorable. The rich culture and well-preserved traditions provided a deep insight into the lives of the local people. Although remote, the accommodations were comfortable and sleeping under the stars was soothing.
author: Lidya Dahung

review_text: Indah dan natural
author: Kamilus Tupen Jumat

review_text:
Waerebo is an interesting tourist spot. This place is very cool and the people here are very kind and friendlly. The Waerebo people live traditionally. And the Waerebo people still preserve their culture very well. Waerebo is famous for its stunning natural beauty and has unique and interesting traditional houses.
I am very happy because I visited this place, basically this place is very cool and really recommended for you to visit.

To visit this place requires a lot of effort because the journey to this place is very far. To visit this place takes around 3-5 hours with uphill terrain.

Even though the journey here is very far and the road is narrow and rocky and beside it is a ravine, the journey is very enjoyable because we are enjoying the interesting views and cool.

Anyway, this place is really cool. Hopefully I can come here again,,,🙌
author: Felin Kartini

review_text: Wae Rebo is one of the most beautiful and captivating tourist destinations, with its stunning natural beauty, cool atmosphere, and very friendly locals. The journey to Wae Rebo from Wae Lomba takes about 3-4 hours. Upon arrival, we were greeted by green mountain scenery and a village atmosphere often enveloped in mist. Interacting with the local residents provides insight into their traditional life and customs. Visitors are required to enter the main traditional house upon arrival and give money to the village chief in Wae Rebo as a token of gratitude to the ancestors (seng wae lu'u).
author: Mita Suriati

review_text:
Beautiful village, in a nice enviroment.

I came just for a short visit during the day. I was asked for 50 000 Idr for "welcome ceremony in the main house" and additional 200 000 Idr as entrance to the village.

250 000 Idr for a short visit feels quite a lot. I tried to negotiate the price, but without success. So I left.

Probably better to stay overnight - night with dinner and breakfast is 325 000, not cheap too, but can be a unique experience.

You don't need any guide, path is pretty easy to follow. Around 1,5 hour up from scooter parking.
author: Petr Kuneš

review_text: Sangat bersejarah
author: Chani J Badpra

review_text:
Je ne donne pas mon avis sur le site traditionnel qui est superbe, belles photos à faire, village bien conservé

Je vous préviens de ce qui s’y passe, nous en revenons tout juste. Et nous aurions aimé connaître ces faits avant de s’y rendre ( ou pas pour le coup, car c’est assez chers pour y aller pour ce qu’il nous attend la bas)

Depuis labuan bajo 4H de route (dont 1h30 dans des chemins horribles, la chauffeur a même creuver une roue). Ne pensez même pas à y aller par vos propres moyens.
Une fois sur place prenez les motos pour vous emmener au maximum qu’ils peuvent. Puis vous aurez à faire 2h30 de trek en montagne avant d’arriver au village.

Dans le village, pas d’échange avec les personnes qui y sont (qui d’ailleurs n’y habitent pas), pas de cérémonie comme nous l’entendons. Il y a seulement une personne qui nous parle dans leur langue durant 1 min.
Ensuite vous attendez sans rien faire de plus jusqu’au soir pour le repas, riz poulet.
Vous dormiez dans une des habitations avec environ 40 autres personnes sur des matelas et des couettes sales. Car les douches se font à la bassine d’eau froide donc pas beaucoup de personnes prennent leur douche… et les draps doivent être lavé tous les 3 mois…
Pas de papiers dans les toilettes donc pensez à en emmener avec vous.
Puis petit déjeuner le lendemain riz œuf dur, et go pour le chemin du retour.

Voilà voilà 😁
author: Antonin Radigois

review_text: Wae Rebo egy skanzenfalu a dél-floresi hegyek közt, különleges, magával ragadó hely, amit az oda vezető út, és a helyi élmények miatt is érdemes meglátogatni. A helyiek befogadóak, a vendégek az egyik autentikus "dob-házban" szállhatnak meg, fontos számukra a közösség, és a harmonikus életmód. Jó, hogy ez a hely még nehezen megközelíthető, hogy el van zárva a nagy tömegek elől.
author: Mátyás Szöllősi

review_text:
Desa adat yang berada diatas ketinggian 1.200 mdpl dan perjalanan untuk kesini memakan waktu 3-5 jam dengan medan yang menanjak. Di desa ini sangat tenang dan menyejukkan juga kita bisa menginap disini selama 1 malam. Untuk bisa kesini kalian harus ikut open trip atau private trip ya karena perjalanan kesini ga mudah dan perlu dibantu oleh tur guide berpengalaman. Sayangnya waktu kesini selama perjalanan hujan dan cukup licin jalannya.
Desa ini sangat indah sekali untuk dikunjungi.

Desa adat waerebo terkenal karena keindahan alamnya yang memukau serta keberadaan rumah adat Mbaru Niang yang unik dan menarik. Masyarakat Waerebo hidup secara tradisional dengan menjaga kelestarian budaya dan lingkungan sekitar. Wisatawan yang berkunjung ke desa ini dapat menikmati keindahan alam pegunungan serta berinteraksi langsung dengan masyarakat setempat yang ramah dan hangat.

Selain itu, Desa Adat Waerebo juga menawarkan pengalaman unik bagi para wisatawan yang ingin merasakan kehidupan tradisional masyarakat Flores. Wisatawan dapat mengikuti berbagai kegiatan seperti memasak makanan tradisional, belajar menenun, dan berpartisipasi dalam upacara adat. Desa ini juga memiliki potensi ekowisata yang dapat memberikan manfaat ekonomi bagi masyarakat setempat. Dengan keindahan alamnya dan keberagaman budayanya, Desa Adat Waerebo menjadi destinasi wisata yang menarik bagi para pengunjung yang ingin merasakan kehidupan tradisional Indonesia. Setidaknya sekali seumur hidup bisa berkunjung ke desa ini. Semoga bisa segera berkunjung lagi ke sini!!!
author: Vera Pujias

review_text:
Untuk menuju ke kampung ini, akan melewati jalan yang rusak parah di sepanjang pantai selatan pulau Flores, sehingga dari Labuan Bajo sampai ke persimpangan menuju ke Waerebo dibutuhkan waktu 4-5 jam.

Kampung Waerebo terletak di atas bukit berketinggian sekitar 1300 mdpl.
Butuh waktu sekitar 2 jam untuk jarak hiking 5,8 km.

Uang masuk per orang Rp. 325.000 dan untuk upacara penyambutan, wisatawan bisa memberikan sumbangan sebesar Rp. 50.000.
Jika membutuhkan seorang guide, maka biayanya Rp. 300.000.
Bagi yang naik ojek dari parkiran mobil, bisa membayar Ro. 100.000 untuk sampai ke titik awal pendakian.
Yang bawa sepeda motor, bisa berkendara sampai di titik awal pendakian, namun hati-hati karena jalannya berlobang dan rusak parah serta sangat terjal.
Informasi lainnya adalah;
Menurut penuturan warga Kampung Waerebo, mereka berasal dari Minangkabau sudah sejak 1000 tahun yang lalu.

review_text:
One of the most memorable experiences in Indonesia. From the trailhead you can leave your motorbike there and give the helmet to the kiosk owner who will look after it for 10k. You can also rent a walking stick for 10k and it’s very helpful for both the climb and descent.

It took me three hours to hike to the village, but a fitter person could do it in two hours. The hike is through the forest so no need to worry about being exposed to the sun. The path is slippery in some places but otherwise in very good condition.

Upon entering the village, you will be escorted to the mandatory welcome in the main house, where you will be asked to pay 50k. And then, if you are sleeping there, it is another 325k for a bed, dinner and breakfast the next day. After that, you’re free to do whatever you want, which is mainly admiring the houses, a short walk around the village and then mingling with the villagers and other guests. For 375k, I think it’s great value for money and by no means extortionate.

They provide a mattress and blanket but you must bring your own towel. The toilet is clean and there is a bucket shower. The bed is comfortable to sleep on, but a few mosquitoes at night. You share the room with other travelers (about 15 during my stay) but it was fine for a night and nice to interact with everyone.

For the dinner and breakfast, they cater for vegetarians. The food is good and filling, I carried a lot of snacks with me, but it was too many. I also carried too many warm clothes - it’s best just to bring one jumper, one raincoat, and 2 x t shirts, jogging bottoms, socks and underwear.

I travelled alone but made good friends with the other guests, that makes the trip so much more memorable. We hiked back to the bottom together the next morning.

The only downside is the road leaving the Wae Rebo is in bad condition, so be prepared for a bumpy ride. I was a bit nervous coming here as a solo traveler, and was considering to get a guide just for company, but it is not necessarily. From the trailhead the hike is not difficult (just tiring) and you will make friends with the other guests.

Thank you Wae Rebo village and I hope to be back some day.
author: Suraj Shah

author: Wira Carita Channel

review_text: Cet endroit est clairement une FUMISTERIE. Ce lieu est, pour la peine, très joli, mais malheureusement est gangrèné par l'argent. Vous ne pourrez pas y mettre un pied sans qu'on vous annonce dès l'entrée la somme de 200.000rp par personne + 50.000rp pour la cérémonie de bienvenue. Quelques mots baragouinés en dialècte local, une traduction récitée et le tour est plié en 4min chrono. Un café et bien gentil. Ils ont également un tampon "PAID" pour joliment accompagner votre facture bien salée. Rarement vu cela en Indonésie. Clairement passé votre chemin, d'autres villages sur Flores sont bien plus intimistes et respectables. La route déglinguée pour y venir est bien plus intéressante que le village lui même.

review_text: Magical village in the valley of a deep mountain. It was out of this world experience, from the trekking, the view and the cultural experience in the village. You will experience living as a local . Sleeping in the communal hut, eating together on the floor and chatting with friendly people in the village. Find pak Benny and pak Agus in one of the hut. They can tell you a lot of stories and history. Come to their hut at 7am in the morning to catch the best sunray for your picture too. And dont forget to try the coffee. It's so good!!

review_text:
Desa terindaahh😍 Aaaahhh~ pengalaman yang tak terlupakan ke desa iniii,, perjalanan dari bajo, dilanjut naik ojek motor, trus tracking yang MasyaAllah bangettt sambil bawa perlengkapan di tas yg ternyata walaupun gk kayak naik gunung dan gk terlalu tinggi MDPL nya tapi bener² berasa tracking nya,,
Perjalanan dari bajo disuguhi pemandangan yg indah, jadi gk berasa lama perjalanan nya..
Jalanan tracking nya ke waerebo campur, ada yg uda bagus, di plur, bebatuan, tanah,,, dari yg lebar jalannya cukup utk 2 orang jalan sampai cuma 1 orang saja dan langsung pinggirnya jurang,, pokokknyaaaa mantep deh ke sinii, pengen balik lagi kesinii, karena setelah perjalanan yg lelah bakal dapet pemandangan yg indah banget yg tak tergantikan...
Masyarakat nya juga baik², tempat penginapan nya, kamar mandinya juga bersih,, pokonya siapapun yg kesini pasti nyaman karena sanitasinya juga sudah okeh, makanannya juga cocok di lidah dan sambelnya khas disana yg pedess banget,,

Waereboo akan kunantikan di perjalanan selanjutnya~
Semoga kita bisa ketemu lagi yahh...🙌

review_text: 17-12-2023 Tempat yg sangat cantik,,,,tapi harus membutuhkan perjuangan untuk sampai tempat ini perjalanan dr bajo di tempuh dlm 4 jam krn medan yg sangat extrime selanjut nya naik ojek skitar 10an menit untuk sampai di pos pendakian krn mobil tdk bs sampai atas jd harus naik ojek'kemudian tracking ke desa adat waerebo dibutuhkan kurang lebih 2 jam perjalanan,,,,jika tdk ingin menginap usahakan pagi sdh naik gunung agar jam 14.00 sdh bisa turun jgn sampai kemalaman krn mengingat jalan yg extrim dan melelahkan,,,tetap semangat " manfaatkan masa muda mu sebelum datang masa tua mu"
author: Eko H

review_text:
It was an incridable experience that I could visit Waerebo! We went to waerebo by motorbike, started 05.00am at Labuan Bajo - We passed the Trans Flores road - Nangalili Village - Denge Village, it took 3.5 hours.

And then, continuing hike around two hours to get to the village. We stayed only 1.5 hours and then we returned to Labuan Bajo, we arrived at hotel around 6pm.
author: hilyatul auliya

review_text: Definitely a beautiful place and an important historical location, but we got nothing out of it other than pictures. After arriving we were greeted by a villager who brought us to the chieftain for a welcome ceremony which took about 5-7 minutes. After that we were told to go to another building where all the tourists were. There we paid for our overnight and dinner (325k per person) and were basically left there. They served a nice simple dinner and breakfast the next morning but there was no integration with the villagers except for those that took our money and served us food. We heard some ceremonial or social gathering in a closed hut during the evening but we weren’t invited to it or even told what it was. They didn’t share anything about the village or their daily life. Aside from pictures we basically spent a lot of money for a 1-star hostel. Not complaining about the sleeping arrangements or anything about the village itself, but we expected to see, or be shown, or even just educated on the life of the villagers, rituals, ceremonies and history. Just a money grab.
author: Michael Chambers

review_text: Environ 3h de scooter de Labuan Bajo sur une route en partie chaotique + jusqu'à 3h de marche. Joli village, paysage fantastique. Cependant nous nous sentons quelque peu harcelés pour l'argent dès notre arrivée. Le prix pour dormir sur place est assez élevé. De plus on nous a réclamé 100 000 de plus car nous sommes tombés un jour de cérémonie. Cérémonie durant laquelle nous avons assisté à la torture d'un coq brûlé vif sur la place publique et en pleine nuit dans le dortoir réservé aux touristes, un cochon a été tranché vivant... Âme sensible à la cause animale s'abstenir ! Après ce sont les coutumes locales... Malgré tout ça vaut la peine d'y faire un tour au moins une nuit.

review_text:
Here are some of the things you can do in Waerebo Village:

• Visit the traditional houses.
Waerebo is known for its unique traditional houses, which are shaped like cones and have seven roofs. The houses are made of bamboo and thatch, and they are decorated with traditional carvings. You can visit the houses and learn about the traditional way of life of the Manggarai people.
• Hike to the village lookout point.
There is a lookout point near the village where you can get stunning views of the surrounding mountains and valleys. The hike to the lookout point is not too difficult, but it is important to wear comfortable shoes and bring water.
• Learn about the Manggarai culture.
The Manggarai people have a rich culture and history. You can learn about the Manggarai culture by visiting the village, attending a traditional dance performance, or talking to the villagers.
• Go trekking in the surrounding area.
There are many trekking trails in the area around Waerebo Village. You can go on a short trek to a nearby waterfall or go on a longer trek through the mountains.
• Relax and enjoy the scenery.
Waerebo Village is a beautiful place to relax and enjoy the scenery. You can sit on a bench and watch the clouds go by, or you can take a walk through the village and enjoy the peace and quiet.

Here are some additional tips for visiting Waerebo Village:

•The village is located in a remote area, so it is important to plan your trip ahead of time. You need to book your accommodation and transportation in advance.
• The best time to visit Waerebo Village is during the dry season, which runs from June to October.
• The village is located at a high altitude, so it is important to pack warm clothes, even if you are visiting during the dry season.
• The villagers are very friendly and welcoming, but it is important to respect their culture and customs. For example, it is considered rude to wear revealing clothing in the village.

Waerebo Village is a great place to experience the traditional culture of the Manggarai people. It is a beautiful and peaceful place, and it is well worth the effort to visit.

Sebelum Sailing Komodo lebih baiknya mengunjungi desa adat Waerebo

Usahakan sampai sebelum jam 11 siang di Ruteng, dr Ruteng ke Dintor (desa terakir) naik Oto Kayu sebutan daerah sana untuk truk yang dimodif untuk angkutan penumpang dan barang dengan lama perjalanan kurang lebih 4 jam

Dari desa Dintor ke Denge menggunakan jasa Ojek

Pokud odmítnete zaplatit vstupné, přestanete je zajímat. Trochu nám to připadalo jako klasicky turistický point. Nic reálného, vše jen naoko. Poradně promyslete návštěvu vesnice.
